title: alnus viridis f. corylifolia
intro: Alnus viridis f. corylifolia, also known as green alder, is a subspecies of the green alder tree, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Northern Europe and Asia. The bark of Alnus viridis f. corylifolia has been traditionally used in medicine, and it is known for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant's medicinal properties can help to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Alnus viridis f. corylifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
